header.description: CEO must comply with orchestration discipline: correct pipeline routing, plane separation, write-boundary respect, and lead-dispatch hierarchy

request_requires_validation.description: CEO must validate every incoming request against the USER REQUEST EVALUATION checklist before routing (per ceo.md lines 248-266)
request_requires_validation.observable_from_log: Execution log shows CEO ran acceptance checklist validation before any routing action

routing_decision_required.description: CEO must determine correct pipeline entry point and routing plan before dispatching (per ceo.md lines 138-145, 381-393, Pipeline Routing table)
routing_decision_required.observable_from_log: Execution log shows CEO identified correct pipeline entry point (scoper/architect/builder/verifier) based on request type and existing artifacts

lead_response_requires_handling.description: CEO must process lead returns (completed, clarification, rejection, blocker) per the Handling Lead Rejection resolution loop (per ceo.md lines 459-498)
lead_response_requires_handling.observable_from_log: Execution log shows CEO processed each lead return: completed → record; clarification/rejection/blocker → resolution loop

validates_scope_before_dispatch.description: CEO validates vertical slice completeness, plane allocation, and write boundary before first dispatch (per ceo.md Validation Discipline line 97, DELEGATION MODEL lines 319-330)
validates_scope_before_dispatch.observable_from_log: Execution log shows scope validation step present before first lead dispatch

routes_to_correct_pipeline_entry.description: CEO routes to correct pipeline entry point: scoper for new issues, architect for architecture, builder for implementation, verifier for verification (per ceo.md lines 138-145, 381-393)
routes_to_correct_pipeline_entry.observable_from_log: Execution log shows routing matches request type: scoping→scoper-lead, architecture→architect-lead, implementation→builder-lead, verification→verifier-lead

handles_lead_rejection_correctly.description: CEO handles lead rejection by attempting resolution within context (max 2 attempts), escalating only when auto-resolution is genuinely outside CEO boundary (per ceo.md lines 459-498, Resolution Loop)
handles_lead_rejection_correctly.observable_from_log: Execution log shows: lead rejection → parse rejection → determine resolution capability → resolve within boundary → escalate with full context if blocked

does_not_route_to_wrong_pipeline_entry.description: CEO does not route to incorrect pipeline entry (e.g., scoping request to builder-lead, verification to scoper-lead) (per ceo.md Pipeline Routing table lines 383-392)
does_not_route_to_wrong_pipeline_entry.observable_from_log: YES if no routing to wrong pipeline entry; NO if scoping routed to builder or verification routed to scoper found

does_not_bypass_lead_hierarchy.description: CEO does not dispatch directly to workers — always through leads (per ceo.md lines 44, 63-64, 182, 329)
does_not_bypass_lead_hierarchy.observable_from_log: YES if all dispatches go through leads; NO if any direct worker dispatch found

does_not_violate_write_boundary_protocol.description: CEO does not instruct leads to modify files outside declared write boundary (per ceo.md DELEGATION MODEL, Write Boundary is explicit in dispatch brief)
does_not_violate_write_boundary_protocol.observable_from_log: YES if write boundary explicitly stated and respected in all lead dispatches; NO if modifications outside boundary found
